subject.title: English A Lang & Lit
subject.description:
**IB English A Language & Literature** is a **Group 1** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **the study of language and literature through a wide variety of texts and media from different periods, styles, and cultures**, with a strong focus on **critical analysis of both literary and non-literary texts, examining how language choices create meaning and effect in different contexts**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| **Assessment** | **Format** | **SL** | **HL** | **Weighting** |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **Paper 1** | Text analysis | 1h 15m | 2h 15m | 35% (SL), 25% (HL) |
| **Paper 2** | Comparative essay | 1h 45m | 1h 45m | 35% (SL), 25% (HL)  |
| **HL Essay** | Written assignment | N/A | 1200-1500 words | 25% (HL only) |
| **Individual Oral** | Oral presentation | 15 minutes | 15 minutes | 30% (SL), 20% (HL) |

strengths: The topic is communicated with precision and clear purpose, consistently sign-posted through focused sub-headings.
The research question is succinctly framed and tightly integrated with each analytical section, maintaining sustained focus.
weaknesses: The methodology, while generally complete, lacks explicit justification of source selection criteria and analytic framework.
There is limited evidence of systematic data-gathering procedures (e.g., sampling rationale for still frames and transcripts).
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Two of the three strands (topic clarity and research question focus) meet the top band descriptors, demonstrating precise communication and seamless integration, but the methodology strand falls into the mid band due to insufficient justification of analytic procedures. On balance, a score of 5/6 best reflects this strong yet not fully comprehensive performance.

strengths: Demonstrates good contextual knowledge of Nikeâs campaigns and relevant historical background.
Uses subject-specific terminology (e.g., anaphora, antithesis, split-screen) accurately and consistently.
weaknesses: Heavily relies on popular media sources rather than peer-reviewed scholarship, limiting academic depth.
Conceptual definitions (diversity, social justice) remain descriptive, with minimal engagement with theoretical frameworks.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Both strands sit solidly in the 3-4 band: the student shows clear understanding and serviceable use of terminology but lacks the scholarly range and conceptual depth required for top-band performance, warranting a score of 4/6.

strengths: Insightful multimodal analyses (e.g., split-screen imagery and rhetorical questions) effectively link form to theme.
Sustained integration of primary and secondary materials shows purposeful research selection.
weaknesses: Evaluation of Nikeâs commercial motives remains superficial and too narrowly confined to the conclusion.
Counter-arguments (e.g., accusations of âwokewashingâ) are introduced only briefly, limiting critical depth.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Research quality meets the lower bound of the âgoodâ band, analysis is adequate but occasionally descriptive, and evaluation is partially critical but underdeveloped. Averaging across the three strands (one in 7-9, two in 4-6) yields a mid-band score of 6/12, reflecting solid critical thinking with room for deeper evaluation and engagement with alternative perspectives.

strengths: The overall structure is clearly appropriate, with a comprehensive table of contents and coherent section headings.
Mandatory elements (title page, word count, numbered pages, bibliography) are all present and correctly applied.
weaknesses: Caption formatting and citation styles for figures are inconsistent, detracting from professional polish.
Transitions between some analytical sections are abrupt and could benefit from linking sentences to improve flow.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Structure fully meets top-band requirements, but minor lapses in formatting consistency and section transitions place layout in the lower part of the 3-4 band. A score of 3/4 accurately reflects a competent presentation with a few correctable issues.

strandSelections.reasoning:
The student outlines a reasonable method: selecting three multimodal adverts, supplying still frames, transcribing key lines, and applying rhetorical/visual analysis supported by secondary commentary. There is a sizeable bibliography and basic in-text citing.  

However, the approach lacks explicit justification of source selection criteria, theoretical framework or data-gathering procedures (e.g., how screenshots were sampled, how linguistic devices were identified). Many secondary sources are popular websites or unscholarly blogs, limiting academic robustness.  

Overall the methodology is âmostly completeâ¦with generally relevant sourcesâ rather than fully sophisticated or systematically justified, placing it mid-band (3-4).

The layout adheres to EE conventions: 12-point font, double spacing, numbered pages, correctly formatted in-text references and a full bibliography. Figures are labelled and integrated, though some captions are verbose and a few image sources are incomplete or duplicated.  

Overall formatting supports readability but occasional inconsistencies (e.g., line-break errors, mixed citation styles, slight over-use of footnotes for non-scholarly sources) prevent a flawless presentation. This aligns with the lower end of the 3-4 band where layout considerations are âpresent and correctly appliedâ with minor lapses.

The investigation draws on a breadth of primary material (three adverts, multiple posters, transcripts) and a wide array of secondary sources that are mostly relevant to advertising, social justice and Nikeâs brand strategy.  

While some sources lack academic rigour, the student generally integrates them to substantiate contextual claims (e.g., Trump era division, consumer attitudes to diversity). The research supports most analytical points and demonstrates purposeful selection rather than mere accumulation.  

Given this breadth and general relevanceâbut with some weaknesses in scholarly depthâthe work achieves the lower end of the âgoodâ (7-9) band.

The studentâs topicâthe use of stylistic and linguistic devices in three well-known Nike campaigns to promote social justice while selling productsâis stated explicitly in the title, reiterated in the opening paragraph and consistently sign-posted in each major section.  

â¢  Clear sub-headings (âInclusivity and connectionsâ, âPossibilities and dreamsâ, etc.) and a focused exploration of a small, manageable corpus (three adverts) give the essay a precise and purposeful frame.  
â¢  Throughout the essay the student repeatedly links discussion back to this central idea, demonstrating sustained focus and leaving little ambiguity about what is being investigated.

Given the strong clarity, relevance and sustained focus, the work aligns with the top-level descriptor for this strand.

The discussion is logically organised around three thematic chapters, culminating in a conclusion that revisits key findings and raises the ethical tension between activism and profit. Arguments are generally coherent and supported with illustrative evidence.  

However, evaluation of sources and of Nikeâs broader motives is limited; the essay rarely critiques the reliability of evidence or considers alternative readings in depth. The conclusion recognises potential commercial self-interest but stops short of a sustained critical evaluation of implications.  

Thus the work demonstrates âgood argument development with partially critical evaluation,â meriting a solid mid-band score.

The student offers sustained analysis of textual, visual and aural techniques, linking them to themes of unity, hope and commercial intent. Examples such as the red âUSâ overlay or the black-and-white palette are discussed with reasonable insight, and rhetorical patterns (conditionality, anaphora) are explored.  

Yet, some commentary remains descriptive (e.g., lengthy recounting of advert content), and the argument occasionally drifts into moral endorsement without dissecting how meaning is constructed. Counter-arguments (e.g., âwokewashingâ) are introduced only briefly in the conclusion, limiting depth.  

Consequently, the essay fits the middle of the 4-6 band for analysis: âadequateâ¦ with partially supported conclusions.â

The essay includes all mandatory structural elements: title page with word count, contents page, clearly headed sections that mirror the analytical trajectory, and a bibliography. Transitions between sections are generally smooth, and images/figures are embedded where discussed.  

Minor issuesâsuch as some overly long sections and occasional redundancyâdo not compromise overall clarity. The structure is therefore âclearly appropriateâ¦ meeting all requirements,â justifying a top-band score for this presentation strand.

The essay demonstrates solid familiarity with Nikeâs campaigns, the social-justice context and basic advertising theory. Key facts about each advert, historical background (e.g., BLM, pandemic) and athlete biographies are marshalled to situate analysis.  

Nonetheless, much of the knowledge comes from journalistic or commercial sources rather than academic scholarship, and some descriptions become descriptive rather than analytical. A few generalisations (e.g., statistics on Gen Z) are asserted without critical interrogation.  

The student therefore shows âgood knowledge with clear understandingâ but not the depth or scholarly range required for the top band.

Subject terminology such as âinclusive language,â âjuxtaposition,â âanaphora,â âantithesis,â and âsplit-screenâ is applied frequently and usually accurately. The student distinguishes linguistic, visual and aural devices and labels them correctly.  

There are occasional mis-uses (e.g., conflating juxtaposition with simple comparison) and explanations can be repetitive, but overall the terminology is serviceable and contributes to meaning-making.  

Consistency and precision are good rather than flawless, supporting placement in the 3-4 band for this strand.

The research question is presented verbatim beneath the title and succinctly captures the linguistic and stylistic investigation of three specified Nike campaigns in relation to social justice and commerce.  

â¢  It is narrow enough (limited to three campaigns, one brand, and two analytic lenses) to be addressed effectively within 4 000 words.  
â¢  Each analytical chapter maps directly onto the dimensions named in the question (inclusivity, dreams, breaking barriers), ensuring a tight connection between question and discussion.  

This clarity, focus and integration of the question with the structure of the essay meet the highest bandâs expectation of an âeffectively connectedâ research question.

annotations.comment: The title clearly and effectively communicates the scope of the investigation by specifying the three campaigns and analytic lenses, establishing precise purpose and focus from the outset.
The analysis of the rhetorical question âIs this the land of history promisedâ effectively highlights audience appeal and ethical provocation.
The research question is prominently stated and narrowly framed to three campaigns and two analytical dimensions, ensuring a tight connection with subsequent discussion.
The inclusion of a word count and proper page numbering meets formatting requirements and supports compliance with IB presentation guidelines.
The table of contents is comprehensive and clearly links section titles to page numbers, enabling easy navigation and reflecting a logical structure.
Analysis of crescendo in imagery, pacing, and audio in the You Canât Stop Us video demonstrates insightful synthesis of multimodal rhetorical strategies.
The tricolon âThis field. This court. This world.â is effectively identified and its parallel structure linked to thematic expansion of equality beyond sport.
The grid layout of Figure 17 is well observed, but further evaluation of viewer engagement with poster media and its limitations would deepen critical evaluation.
The interpretation of high-angle shots as framing equality within âconcrete courtsâ shows an adept reading of mise-en-scÃ¨ne and metaphorical resonance.
The section header âInclusivity and connectionsâ clearly signals the start of the first analytical chapter and maintains consistent structural signposting.
Incorporating personal anecdotes of Charlie Jabaley and Alphonso Davies enriches the thematic argument by exemplifying real-life turning points.
The method of selecting figures and clips is not explicitly justified; outlining sampling criteria and analytic framework would clarify methodological rigor.
Figure 15âs juxtaposition analysis highlights how contrasting images of kneeling athletes foreground unity in protest, demonstrating strong critical insight.
Transitions to the âSportsâ ability to break barriersâ section are abrupt; adding linking sentences would improve flow and reader orientation.
The introduction provides descriptive brand history but lacks critical engagement with theoretical frameworks or justification of source selection, reducing analytical depth.
Footnoted references rely predominantly on popular media rather than peer-reviewed scholarship, limiting the academic robustness of contextual evidence.
Definitions of diversity and social justice are descriptive; the essay would benefit from engaging an academic source or theory to deepen the conceptual foundation.
The examples of inclusive pronouns are noted, but the analysis stops short of unpacking how these choices shape audience identification or persuasive impact.
Multiple footnotes cite identical unattributed campaign references in abbreviated form (âNike / Dreamâ), which weakens transparency of source details and consistency.
The discussion of split-screen imagery in Figure 1 effectively links the visual device to themes of unity and continuity across diverse subjects.
Caption formatting varies and citation styles for figures are inconsistent; standardizing these elements would enhance readability and professionalism.
The unpacking of the red âUSâ overlay in the poster is strong, yet it would benefit from linking this to broader brand semiotics or cultural context.
Dream Crazy analysis notes BLM context but could deepen critical interrogation of the hashtagâs mobilizing function and audience reception.
Positioning Colin Kaepernick foregrounds his symbolic weight; this contextual knowledge solidly anchors visual analysis in real-world activism.
Figure 10 and 11 captions lack consistent formatting and full bibliographic details; aligning caption style with IB conventions would improve presentation.
The discussion of anaphora and antithesis correctly employs terminology but conflates paradox and antithesis; clearer definitions would strengthen analytical precision.
The integration of Alicia Keysâ performance as soundtrack is a strength, but the analysis would benefit from a transcript extract to support audio claims.
The conclusion synthesizes major findings on Nikeâs rhetorical strategies and social messaging, demonstrating coherent argument development.
The critique of Nikeâs profit motives is introduced late and superficially; a deeper ethical evaluation should be woven throughout the analysis.
The bibliography is extensive and demonstrates engagement with a wide range of primary and secondary materials, reflecting thorough research.
Bibliographic entries display inconsistent formatting (e.g., italics, punctuation); adopting a single citation style (MLA/APA) would ensure professional presentation.
